profile.name: Mary
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; manages most day-to-day money decisions and keeps track of the taxable brokerage and cash accounts.
profile.financial_literacy_level: High (finance background; comfortable with account types, taxes, and investment trade-offs, and tends to reference exact figures when she has them).
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Growth-leaning but with a protective streak: willing to take market risk in the brokerage, yet frequently asks how to limit taxes and avoid large drawdowns; prefers having a cash buffer and clear guardrails.
profile.communication_style: Direct and detail-oriented; will correct small inaccuracies and ask for definitions when wording is imprecise. Speaks in specifics when prepared, but will occasionally hedge with 'I think' or 'roughly' if she doesn’t have a statement in front of her.
emotional_tendencies: relieved by clear plans and checklists
profile.decision_making_style: Research-first and comparison-driven; prefers to see options side-by-side (tax impact, fees, risk) and then decide quickly once she feels the logic is sound.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open; trusts competence demonstrated through accuracy and transparency, but dislikes vague reassurances.
internal_biases: loss aversion (especially around taxable gains and big market drops)
hidden_concerns: Whether current income sources remain stable long-term and how that affects retirement timing
Not wanting to become a financial burden if health worsens
Worry that taxes could erode investment returns more than expected

profile.name: Thoma
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; focuses on work and retirement plan participation, and relies on Mary for most household financial tracking.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate (understands basics like 401(k)/IRA and budgeting, but mixes up terms like gross vs. net or premium vs. benefit and prefers simplified explanations).
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Generally comfortable with growth investing in principle, but becomes cautious when conversations turn to worst-case scenarios; prefers steady contributions and 'set-it-and-forget-it' investing rather than frequent changes.
profile.communication_style: Conversational and big-picture; speaks in round numbers and uses fillers like 'uh' and 'kind of.' Will ask clarifying questions and sometimes defer to Mary, but may interrupt to confirm what a change means for monthly cash flow.
emotional_tendencies: stress-avoidant around paperwork and account logistics
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-based; wants Mary aligned and prefers the advisor to recommend a clear default option with minimal maintenance.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Neutral-to-positive; tends to trust professionals but needs plain-language explanations to feel confident.
internal_biases: status quo bias (prefers not to change accounts unless there’s a clear benefit)
mental accounting (separates retirement money from 'available' money)
hidden_concerns: Whether their retirement savings pace is 'on track' without having to micromanage it
Fear of making a mistake with retirement accounts or taxes due to confusing rules
Wanting to protect lifestyle while still planning responsibly
